title: Sort & Multi-field Search
description: Add sorting and improved search to the contact manager. Create sort_contacts() that returns contacts sorted by a given field, and search_contacts() that searches across all fields (name, phone, email).
starter_code:
# Contact Manager — Step 5: Sort & Multi-field Search
# Use list methods and dict operations for sorting and searching.

contacts = [
    {'name': 'Charlie', 'phone': '555-0003', 'email': 'charlie@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Alice', 'phone': '555-0001', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'phone': '555-0002', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
]

def sort_contacts(contact_list, field='name'):

    Field can be 'name', 'phone', or 'email'. Case-insensitive for strings.
    """

    return []

def search_contacts(contact_list, query):
    """Search all fields (name, phone, email) for the query string.
    Case-insensitive. Returns list of matching contacts.
    """

    return []

# Test it
print("Sorted by name:")
for c in sort_contacts(contacts):
    print(f"  {c['name']}")

print(f"\nSearch for '555': {len(search_contacts(contacts, '555'))} results")
print(f"Search for 'alice': {len(search_contacts(contacts, 'alice'))} results")
test_code:
test_contacts = [
    {'name': 'Charlie', 'phone': '555-0003', 'email': 'charlie@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Alice', 'phone': '555-0001', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'phone': '555-0002', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
]
sorted_result = sort_contacts(test_contacts, 'name')
assert isinstance(sorted_result, list), 'sort_contacts should return a list'
assert len(sorted_result) == 3, 'sort_contacts should return all contacts'
assert sorted_result[0]['name'] == 'Alice', f"First sorted by name should be Alice, got {sorted_result[0]['name']}"
assert sorted_result[2]['name'] == 'Charlie', f"Last sorted by name should be Charlie, got {sorted_result[2]['name']}"
assert test_contacts[0]['name'] == 'Charlie', 'sort_contacts should not modify the original list'
sorted_phone = sort_contacts(test_contacts, 'phone')
assert sorted_phone[0]['phone'] == '555-0001', 'Should sort by phone field when specified'
results = search_contacts(test_contacts, '555')
assert len(results) == 3, f'Searching for 555 should match all 3, got {len(results)}'
results2 = search_contacts(test_contacts, 'alice')
assert len(results2) == 1, f'Searching for alice should match 1, got {len(results2)}'
results3 = search_contacts(test_contacts, 'example.com')
assert len(results3) == 3, f'Searching for example.com should match all 3, got {len(results3)}'
results4 = search_contacts(test_contacts, 'CHARLIE')
assert len(results4) == 1, 'Search should be case-insensitive'
results5 = search_contacts(test_contacts, 'zzz')
assert len(results5) == 0, 'Search for nonexistent should return empty list'
acceptance_criteria: sort_contacts() returns a new sorted list without modifying original
Sorting works for any field: name, phone, email
search_contacts() matches across all fields (name, phone, email)
Returns empty list when no matches
hints: Use sorted(contact_list, key=lambda c: c[field].lower()) for case-insensitive sort
For multi-field search, check if query.lower() appears in any value of each contact dict
Loop through c.values() to check all fields: any(query.lower() in v.lower() for v in c.values())
